items.speaker: Luther
items.text: A place this intricate is difficult to fully comprehend in such a short time. You seem to know this place like the back of your hand. Have you done some research prior to your last visit?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Not really. A lot of it came from... the person that was with me at the time. She was one of the founders of this place.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: I see. One moment, I'll add a few final notes here.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The Fallen Grave relies heavily on frequency decomposers. Only a small number of Echoes maintained their operation, and no personnel appeared during the course of the investigation. Future management may require fewer staff.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Evaluating and handling anomalies in the Common Echoes requires further revision. They should be offered a choice, rather than helplessly be sent on a conveyor belt for disassembly and smelting.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Are we now inside the Fabricatorium? This area is...

options.text: We are in the Fallen Grave.
It's a deeper part of the Fabricatorium.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Coldness and death litter these obscure corners.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Only a few straggler Echoes remain, watching their own kind being fed into the "furnace" one by one.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: We've always regarded Common Echoes as gifts from the Sentinel, never caring about their birth or death. But here lies the dissolving and decaying truth behind the miracles.

options.text: The frequency will be recycled once broken down.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: You're saying frequency energy can be reused? You mean there's a way for these abnormal Echoes to be reborn after meeting their end?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Where does that energy lead? Do the reborn Echoes retain their past consciousness?

options.text: Let's keep moving. The depths will answer your questions.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: You speak in riddles. I suppose I'll have to take notes more carefully.

options.text: (Why am I starting to talk like this too...?)

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Strange. The melody of this place has gone back to normal. The Echoes shouldn't be behaving like this.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: ...It seems you haven't reached the deeper truth. This place is more than just a factory for Echoes.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The new research data I gathered indicates the Order has been secretly pushing experiments on Echo consciousness control in the facility.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: In other words, if things had gone according to the Order's plans, the Echoes would become mere puppets and watchdogs entirely under their control.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: But... we should've led with faith, not attempting control through force.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: If all we have is sand slipping through our fingers, no matter how tightly we grasp, nothing would have mattered. Why, Primus Fenrico, why must you do this...

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Ah, apologies, I tend to talk to myself sometimes... Let's move on and document this place.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The amount of energy stored here is astounding.

options.text: Energy Hub serves as the central energy storage.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The data shows two types of energy being stored here.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: One of them matches what we found in the Fallen Grave. Recycled Echo frequencies must be stored here before being distributed to the incubation areas.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The other energy source appeared on devices we saw before. It seems to be the power source that supports the Fabricatorium's operations.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: But why is it giving a low energy level reading...

options.text: Must be because of the explosion.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Explosion?

options.text: We had to detonate some of the energy to escape from underground.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: So there's an underground section of the facility. And that's where the energy storage area is located?

options.text: Yes, but I'm not sure if there's still a way down there.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Let's go take a look.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: These Echoes have less stable consciousness than ordinary Common Echoes. The experiment here is truly a chore.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: It's not wise to hold everything too tightly. Sometimes, letting go brings greater rewards. An experiment that does only harm may not be worth continuing...

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: As it stands, Ragunna still needs a place where the Echoes can call home...

options.text: There's one last thing I need to tell you.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This is the control hub of the entire Fabricatorium, overseeing all of its areas. Each region has a similar device that reacts to every change.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: I've noticed and recorded an image of it.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Someone once told me that everything is made of frequencies, melodies can bend the rhythm of existence, and by orchestrating their essence, we reshape their very nature.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The key to Fabricatorium's operations is a melody. Keep this melody, make sure it plays well, and the Fabricatorium will remain stable.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: If you want to make a change here, you'll have to adjust the melody. No way around it.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Do you remember the melody?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I do.
You hum the melody softly, and its tune echoes throughout the tower.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: What a beautiful song. As if I can almost hear a story from it.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: I have it recorded. Seeing how focused you are, I took the liberty of capturing your image.

options.text: It's fine, as long as it helps you.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Your guidance has been essential to my understanding of this place. Trust me, we will make this place better.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The age of lies is behind us now. Now that the shadow over the Order has lifted, I'm sure the brighter days will come.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Hmm? Why are you looking at me like that?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Heh, isn't that good? Ambition, or you might call it, a desire for the better.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The Order of the Deep needs change. Ragunna deserves a better future, but who will lead that change, I'm not sure.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Luther gave you a calm and meaningful smile before bowing deeply in gratitude.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Looks like the path is sealed. We'll need someone to repair the underground levels first.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Hmm? What are these?

options.text: Seems like someone managed to salvage some files from below.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: They are not entirely about Echoes. And the data seems to be from another era.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: No, wait, given the age of these records, the conclusions about the Waveworn Phenomenon were far ahead of their time.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Mind if we look over this again? I want to make sure we don't miss any critical information.
